texto: We don’t even survive in the memories of the living. Science has destroyed that myth. Whenever we remember something, what we’re doing is remembering the last time we remembered it; our memory doesn’t go back to the original notch, the first one was cut, but to the last one. Human memory is virtual, like that of a computer. When we open a file we’re not opening it as it was when we first created it, but as it was the last time we used it. It is called hypercathexis and is our brain’s most sophisticated recourse when it comes to confronting pain.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Enrique de Hériz: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “We don’t even survive in the memories of the living. Science has destroyed that myth. Whenever we…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, memories, memory pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de dia-chuvoso e reflexao.

frase_original: We don’t even survive in the memories of the living. Science has destroyed that myth. Whenever we remember something, what we’re doing is remembering the last time we remembered it; our memory doesn’t go back to the original notch, the first one was cut, but to the last one. Human memory is virtual, like that of a computer. When we open a file we’re not opening it as it was when we first created it, but as it was the last time we used it. It is called hypercathexis and is our brain’s most sophisticated recourse when it comes to confronting pain.

texto: Serena has spent her life fighting fiction the way good soldiers fight—intent on detecting its presence, harassing it, suppressing it—but I have to find a way to show her she’s mistaken her enemy, to explain to her that whoever suppresses fiction destroys life, and that everything disappears with it, all love, all desire. If the past is an invention, it’s not such a big deal. After all, the future’s an invention, and no one finds that hard to accept.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Enrique de Hériz sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. A afirmação “Serena has spent her life fighting fiction the way good soldiers fight—intent on detecting its pr…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, future, storytelling, útil em momentos de storytelling.

If the past is an invention, it’s not such a big deal. After all, the future’s an invention, and no one finds that hard to accept.

texto: …nostalgia is, by definition, the least authentic of all feelings.
